title: Open-issues backlog clearance: M1 stale-issue sweep + M2 EXP-tied auto-close + M3 carry-over enforcement, with burn-down vs BAU mode controls
authored_by: tinker (2026-05-10 run, in response to DIRECTIVE-20260510-001)
target: monitor/prompts/decider.md (Priority 5b new section + Step E5/E6 mode handling) + monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md (Step 8 EXP-tied auto-close addendum) + monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md (carry-over enforcement) + monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md (Step 8 carry-over annotation schema) + monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (new fields, closure-ledger schema) + new files monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl + monitor/decisions/decider-mode.json + revert script in build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js

executive_summary.problem_one_line: 231 open issues are aging in open-issues.json with no dispatcher slot to walk the parking lot — the 30-day OBE threshold is days away for the oldest item.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Three structural mechanisms that route the parking-lot to the decider's normal flow (M1 sweep + M2 EXP-tied auto-close + M3 carry-over enforcement), plus a burn-down/BAU toggle so the existing 231-item backlog can drain in days under elevated caps and tighter safety while everything else stays steady-state.

tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_mechanism_scope: Implement all three (M1+M2+M3). They address different leak modes; cutting any one leaves a leak.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_burndown_engagement: Flag file `monitor/decisions/decider-mode.json` (`{"mode":"burndown"|"bau"}`). Operator HNOTE optional escape hatch. Auto-revert when `count(open) < 50` for 3 consecutive runs OR after 7 elapsed days, whichever first.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_safety_controls_sufficient: Yes, with one addition: ALL burn-down closures carry `auto_closed: true, closed_by_run: <ID>, ledger_entry: <line>` so the revert script has a single source of truth.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_caps: BAU=10/run cap (cheap, leaves headroom for normal work). Burn-down=30/run cap (3x BAU, drains 200 items in ~7 runs at 60% hit-rate ≈ ~1.5 days at 6 runs/day).
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q5_curmudgeon_interaction: Curmudgeon keeps running. The burn-down bound is per-decider-run, not pipeline-global. New ISS creation by curmudgeon is welcome; M3 (carry-over enforcement) prevents the 'one-step-forward-one-back' churn where new ISSs immediately age.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q6_M2_false_positive_guard: Three-rule gate: (a) severity ∈ {minor,info} only — moderate/major never auto-close on EXP signal alone; (b) no 'deferred|blocks|needs:|when pipeline|remaining|backfill|DO NOT INTEGRATE|awaiting human' substring in description (negative-signal blocklist); (c) source/found_by NOT in {curmudgeon, curmudgeon-review} AND source path NOT under monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ (curmudgeon-found holes on the EXP are SEPARATE from 'EXP integrated' status).
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q7_M3_schema: Add `unresolved_prior_cycle: [{ iss_id, prior_cycle, holes_index }]` array on curmudgeon review JSON. Decider Priority 3 reads this array directly and treats listed ISSs as MUST-process in the same run. Free-text 'still open from c-N' in description is preserved as a fallback for legacy reviews and human readers.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q8_implementation_order: Order C (phased with burn-down first phase). Phase 1: M2 + decider-mode.json + closure-ledger.jsonl + dry-run-by-default for 24h. Phase 2 (after ~48h of operator-confirmed clean closures): M1 + M3 land in BAU. Rationale below.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q9_24_stale_statused_entries: One-shot archiver to closed-issues.json AS PART OF Phase 1 (cheap, no review required since these statuses already say 'this is done'). Specifically: status ∈ {closed, fixed, wontfix} → archive immediately; status ∈ {monitoring, blocked-on-curmudgeon, assigned-analyst, partially-fixed-pending-verification, pending-human} → leave in place (these are truly active, just non-'open').

current_state_open_issues_json.directive_said_24_terminal_actually_26: Within rounding — directive filed 50min before tinker re-measured; 2 may have flipped status in between
current_state_open_issues_json.directive_said_88_actually_90: Same explanation — re-measure shows 90; both are accurate-to-the-minute snapshots.

age_distribution_open_issues.note_on_undated: 27 open issues lack `found_at`/`found_date` fields entirely (e.g., ISS-1245, ISS-1246, ISS-1043). These are schema-non-conformant and need a separate schema-cleanup pass — flagged here, deferred to a separate PROP. M1's age computation must handle them by either (a) defaulting to 'unknown — manual review required' or (b) inferring from creation order vs ID-vs-date-anchored neighbors. Recommend (a).

carry_over_signal_in_descriptions.note: Free-text matching is brittle (the four observed phrasings aren't exhaustive). M3 schema bump is the right structural fix.

oldest_open_issue.description_excerpt: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file…

M1_stale_issue_sweep.summary: Add Priority 5b (between current Priority 5 and the End-of-Run steps) to decider.md: each run, scan open-issues.json for items with age > N days; for each, take ONE of: (a) patch (if scope-appropriate and small), (b) wontfix-with-rationale (if no longer relevant per current codebase state), (c) escalate to operator-required by adding `escalation_reason` and tagging `status: 'pending-human'`. Cap at K items per run.
M1_stale_issue_sweep.what_problem_does_it_solve: Catches anything that fell through M2 and M3 — the durable safety net. Also handles the long tail of decider-self-tagged 'lower priority than content work' items that quietly age forever (ISS-708 archetype).

N_age_threshold.rationale: 14 is too aggressive — items legitimately can age 14d if the curmudgeon's next review of a target is genuinely deep work that hasn't fired yet. 28 is too lax — by 28d the item has missed multiple curmudgeon cycles AND is approaching OBE. 21 is the inflection: an item still-open at 21d has either (a) been actively deferred by decider on multiple runs, (b) been forgotten, or (c) is genuinely waiting on external work — M1's job is to surface (b) and (c) explicitly.

frequency.rationale: The cap (K=10 in BAU, K=30 in burn-down) provides the throttle. Running every run avoids 'oh I missed the sweep window today' degenerate case.

K_per_run_cap.rationale: BAU=10: ~5min of decider context-budget at typical-issue size, leaves headroom for normal Priority 1-5 work. burn_down=30: 3x. At 60% effective-close rate × 30 cap × 6 runs/day = ~108 closures/day. Backlog of 200 drains in ~2 days.

severity_tier_action_matrix.minor: Auto-act allowed: patch (small) | wontfix-with-rationale (if codebase context says no-longer-real) | escalate (otherwise).
severity_tier_action_matrix.moderate: Auto-act with a higher bar: patch only if the patch is exactly find/replace-mechanical and scope is narrow; otherwise escalate to pending-human (do NOT auto-wontfix).
severity_tier_action_matrix.major: Never auto-close. Only action allowed: re-route via pending-human with explicit rationale OR file a fresh EXP if scope demands.

is_this_still_real_signal.primary: Re-grep the codebase for the specific text/condition the ISS describes. If the text/condition no longer exists OR has been substantively rewritten since the ISS was created, mark as 'no-longer-real' candidate.
is_this_still_real_signal.secondary: If the ISS references an EXP-NNN that has since integrated (M2 territory), defer to M2 logic — don't double-count.
is_this_still_real_signal.tertiary: If the curmudgeon has touched the target in a later cycle without re-flagging the same issue, presume the issue is no longer active (the curmudgeon would have re-flagged if it were). Conservative: only takes effect if the later cycle's review file `holes_found` array does NOT contain a description match.
is_this_still_real_signal.fallback_when_signals_conflict: Default to escalation (pending-human), not auto-wontfix. Ambiguity always loses to operator visibility.

where_it_plugs_in.section: After current 'Priority 5 — Standard Processing' (line ~119), before 'Every run — Patches and Reporting' (line ~123)
where_it_plugs_in.new_section_body_skeleton: Read decider-mode.json → if mode='burndown' set K=30, else K=10. Run query: open issues with age > 21d, sorted oldest-first, capped at K. For each: read curmudgeon's most recent cycle review of the target; re-grep the live file for the specific issue text. Choose action per severity tier matrix. For closures: append to closure-ledger.jsonl, move from open-issues.json to closed-issues.json, mark `auto_closed_by_M1: true`. For escalations: set status='pending-human', add `escalation_reason`. Log decisions in daily report under `m1_sweep` field.

M1_stale_issue_sweep.estimated_close_rate: ≈55-70% on the existing backlog. Most of the ≤21d→>21d cohort that's been aging quietly is either (a) covered by an integrated EXP (M2 reaches it first), (b) addressed by curmudgeon's later cycle (no-longer-real signal), or (c) genuinely deferrable minor housekeeping (auto-wontfix-with-rationale). The remaining ~30-45% legitimately need operator escalation.

M2_exp_tied_auto_close.summary: Whenever decider integrates an EXP-NNN (Step 2a in decider-curmudgeon.md), additionally search open-issues.json for any ISS where (a) `related_expansion === 'EXP-NNN'` OR (b) the description/source/source_file contains the substring 'EXP-NNN' AND the ISS passes the false-positive guard. Auto-close matched ISSs with reason='EXP-NNN integrated by run XXX, ISS no longer relevant'.
M2_exp_tied_auto_close.what_problem_does_it_solve: Closes ISSs that were created as forwarding/handoff tickets ('EXP-NNN ready — pending curmudgeon review') or as scope-trackers ('EXP-NNN waiting on infrastructure') that lose their reason for being once the EXP integrates. Catches the ~15-25 ISSs that wait silently for an EXP that has long since landed.
M2_exp_tied_auto_close.directive_says_88_match_substring: The naive substring-match catches 90 (re-measured) ISSs, BUT — see worked example — only ~5-10 of those would actually pass the false-positive guard. M2's value is the GUARD, not the count of substring matches.

what_constitutes_a_match.secondary_signal: Substring 'EXP-NNN' present in any of: description, title, source, source_file, notes
what_constitutes_a_match.rejected: Looking up issue_ids on the EXP itself — that's the EXISTING flow (Step 8 of EXP integration already closes those). M2 is for the orphan ISSs that DIDN'T get included in `issue_ids`.

scope_of_auto_close.rule: Apply to all open ISSs that pass the false-positive guard, regardless of current pseudo-status (e.g., 'pending curmudgeon review', 'pending integration', 'open').
scope_of_auto_close.reason: If the EXP integrated AND the ISS passes the FP guard, the ISS is by definition no-longer-relevant. Don't filter by status.

false_positive_guard.headline_rule: Three-rule conjunctive gate. Auto-close ONLY if ALL three hold:
false_positive_guard.rule_1_severity_floor: issue.severity ∈ {minor, info}. Moderate/major/critical never auto-close on EXP signal alone (they always require operator review).
false_positive_guard.rule_2_negative_signal_blocklist: issue.description (case-insensitive) does NOT match any of: 'deferred|deferred from|deferred AC|blocks integration|needs:|when pipeline|remaining for future|backfill needed|DO NOT INTEGRATE|awaiting human|future work|still needs|pending integration|requires (?!.* completed)'. The negation in the last term ('requires X' is OK if 'requires X completed' — i.e., already done). The blocklist is a positive signal that the ISS describes work the EXP did NOT close.
false_positive_guard.rule_3_source_provenance: issue.found_by NOT in {curmudgeon, curmudgeon-review, curmudgeon-digest} AND issue.source path NOT under 'monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/'. Curmudgeon-found holes on an EXP are SEPARATE issues — the EXP integration didn't close them. (Distinct from the ISSs created when EXP integration RESOLVES a curmudgeon-found hole — those are typically closed in Step 8 by issue_ids and never become orphans).
false_positive_guard.fallback_when_uncertain: If rules 1-3 all pass but you're uncertain, escalate to dry-run-list-only (write a candidate-closure-list to ledger but don't actually close). Operator approves batch on next run. In burn-down Phase 1, ALL closures default to dry-run for first 24h.

trigger_point.every_EXP_integration: Yes — built into Step 2a so it runs on every integration. No need for `closes_iss: [...]` field on EXPs.
trigger_point.rationale: The whole point is to catch orphans the EXP forgot to list. Requiring an explicit field on the EXP would replicate the same forgetting-mode.

where_it_plugs_in.section: Step 8 (Close related issues — do NOT skip), at line ~176
where_it_plugs_in.addendum_skeleton: After existing Step 8 closes the ISSs in `issue_ids`: additionally call M2 helper: for each open ISS in open-issues.json, check related_expansion match OR substring match against EXP-NNN. For each match, apply false-positive guard (rules 1-3). For each ISS that passes the guard: append to closure-ledger.jsonl with `closure_reason: 'M2_exp_tied'`, move to closed-issues.json with status='fixed', `fixed_by: 'M2-auto-close'`, `closed_by_run: <decider_run_id>`, `auto_closed: true`. Log count in daily report `m2_auto_close.count`.

worked_examples_from_current_open_issues._intro: These six examples span the false-positive landscape — three pass the guard for auto-close, three fail. The descriptions/severities are real (re-measured 2026-05-10).

example_1_SAFE_to_auto_close.title: EXP-249 (Phase B1 pie chart cleanup) ready — pending curmudgeon review
example_1_SAFE_to_auto_close.description_excerpt: Analyst delivered EXP-249-phase-b1-pie-chart-cleanup.json. Six coupled changes in build-scripts/generate-html.js…
example_1_SAFE_to_auto_close.verdict: AUTO-CLOSE — pure forwarding/handoff ticket, EXP-249 has integrated, the work flagged here is done.

example_2_NOT_safe_post_integration_defe.description_excerpt: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file… When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML…
example_2_NOT_safe_post_integration_defe.rule_2_negative_signal: FAIL — description contains 'When pipeline supports' (future-work signal)
example_2_NOT_safe_post_integration_defe.verdict: DO NOT AUTO-CLOSE — the EXP integrated but the ISS describes followup work the EXP explicitly did not perform. Route through M1 sweep instead (currently 29.5d → eligible for M1 if M1's signal-matrix concludes 'no-longer-real' or escalates if not).

example_3_NOT_safe_curmudgeon_found_hole.title: EXP-190: provenance-tag classification rule subjective
example_3_NOT_safe_curmudgeon_found_hole.description_excerpt: H1 (major) from EXP-190-proposal.json curmudgeon review… Blocks integration
example_3_NOT_safe_curmudgeon_found_hole.verdict: DO NOT AUTO-CLOSE — triple-fail. This is a curmudgeon-found hole on the EXP-190 proposal package, NOT a forwarding ticket. EXP-190 hasn't even integrated yet.

example_4_NOT_safe_explicit_deferral.description_excerpt: EXP-211 integration deferred AC8/9/10/12 (Playwright reachability and a11y assertions). test.js has no Playwright integration; AC8/9/10… were explicitly deferred at integration time.
example_4_NOT_safe_explicit_deferral.exp_status_in_archive: EXP-211 IS integrated (partially — AC8/9/10/12 explicitly deferred)
example_4_NOT_safe_explicit_deferral.verdict: DO NOT AUTO-CLOSE — explicit-deferral ticket, the EXP integrated but the ISS scopes work the integration explicitly carved out. Route through M1 sweep at 21d threshold.

example_5_NOT_safe_design_taste_disagree.description_excerpt: Optional aggregate kill-shot sentence… Analyst says per-WIN enumeration in EXP-170 is already better; this is stylistic. Linked: EXP-170
example_5_NOT_safe_design_taste_disagree.rule_2_negative_signal: PASS (no blocklist substring — though 'this is stylistic' is interesting; could add 'stylistic|optional' to the blocklist?)
example_5_NOT_safe_design_taste_disagree.verdict_under_proposed_rules: AUTO-CLOSE — but this is a borderline case. The ISS argues 'per-WIN enumeration in EXP-170 is already better' — i.e., EXP-170's integration is essentially the analyst's own answer to whether to add this sentence. Reasonable to close. If operator wants to be more conservative, add 'optional|stylistic|sidestepped' to the negative-signal blocklist (rule 2) — that would route this to M1 instead.
example_5_NOT_safe_design_taste_disagree.tinker_recommendation: Auto-close as proposed (analyst's own rationale says EXP-170 supersedes this). Don't expand blocklist to 'optional|stylistic' — would cause too many false-NOT-close misses.

example_6_NOT_safe_proposal_phase_block.title: Scoreboard Phase 1 draft — awaiting human-group review (DO NOT INTEGRATE)
example_6_NOT_safe_proposal_phase_block.verdict: DO NOT AUTO-CLOSE — explicit human-review hold, EXP not integrated.

summary_classification_of_six_examples.auto_close_yield_estimate: Of the 90 EXP-mentioning open issues (re-measured), the rules estimate ~10-20 will auto-close cleanly. The remaining 70+ are either (a) post-integration deferrals (M1 territory), (b) curmudgeon-found holes on EXPs (require curmudgeon's next cycle to clear), or (c) major/critical (always operator review).

M3_carryover_enforcement.summary: When a curmudgeon writes a c-(N+1) review listing 'still open from c-N' issues, decider treats those listed ISSs as MUST-process in the SAME run that integrates the c-(N+1) review. The signal is a new structured field on the curmudgeon review JSON.
M3_carryover_enforcement.what_problem_does_it_solve: The c4→c5→c6 ratchet (e.g., WIN-053 cluster, ISS-1244/1245/1246) where curmudgeon politely re-flags the same issues for cycles and decider keeps reading them as 'oh, those again' and not acting. Forces a binary patch-or-wontfix-with-rationale decision per carry-over.

schema.where_curmudgeon_writes_it: Step 8 of curmudgeon.md (review writing). When the curmudgeon flags a hole that was first raised in an earlier cycle, it adds an entry to this array — automatically populated by the curmudgeon recognizing the 'still open from c-N' pattern in its own draft prose, OR (more reliably) by cross-checking the holes_found of the prior-cycle review file at write time.
schema.fallback_for_legacy_reviews: Decider's Priority 3 carryover-detector first reads the structured field; if absent, falls back to free-text grep of holes_found descriptions for /still open from c\d+|carry[- ]?(over|forward) c\d+|deferred from EXP-/i. This preserves existing review file readability for ISSs already in flight.
schema.rationale_for_structured_over_freetext: Free-text 'still open from cN' is brittle (already three different phrasings in current corpus: 'still open from c4', 'carry-forward c1/c2/c3', 'deferred from EXP-228'). The structured field eliminates the parsing bug-surface.

decider_required_action_per_carry_over.rule: Each carry-over ISS in `unresolved_prior_cycle` MUST get exactly one of three terminal actions in the SAME run that integrates the c-(N+1) review:
decider_required_action_per_carry_over.action_b: Wontfix-with-rationale — explicit reasoning for not patching, written into the closure record. NOT a punt.
decider_required_action_per_carry_over.action_c: Operator escalation — set status='pending-human' with escalation_reason field. Use when patching requires deeper work than a single decider run can budget for.
decider_required_action_per_carry_over.what_is_NOT_allowed: Skipping the carry-over without a recorded action. The current bug is precisely 'no-action-taken' as a hidden default.

no_longer_relevant_path.rule: If decider re-greps and finds the prior-cycle finding is no longer present in the live target (e.g., the section was rewritten and the issue moot), action_b applies with rationale 'no-longer-real per re-grep at <run-id>'. This is the explicit 'wontfix with carryover-rationale' path Q's M3 design questions ask for.

interaction_with_curmudgeon_batch_gate_P.rule: PROP-025 (curmudgeon batch-gate, currently proposed) classifies queue items as `verification` (batchable) vs `deep-attack` (singleton). Carry-overs do NOT change classification — a verification-class push that includes carry-overs is still verification-class. But DECIDER's response to a verification-class review must include carry-over enforcement: even on a 'just verify the patch landed' cycle, any unresolved-prior-cycle items must still get the patch/wontfix/escalate triage in the same run.
interaction_with_curmudgeon_batch_gate_P.rationale: Decoupling: PROP-025 governs HOW MUCH work the curmudgeon does per run; M3 governs WHAT decider must do with whatever the curmudgeon says is still open. Independent axes.

curmudgeon_side.section: Step 8 review writing (around the holes_found section)
curmudgeon_side.change: Add: 'When a hole you're flagging was first raised in an earlier cycle on the same target, also append to `unresolved_prior_cycle: [{iss_id, prior_cycle, holes_index}]` at the top level of the review JSON. The iss_id is the open-issues.json ID, the prior_cycle is the earliest cycle in which this finding was raised, and holes_index is the 0-based index in your current holes_found array (so decider can cross-walk).'

decider_side.section: Step 8 (Close related issues), at line ~176, AFTER the existing 'issue_ids' close logic AND AFTER the M2 EXP-tied auto-close addendum
decider_side.change: Add: 'After M2: read `unresolved_prior_cycle[]` from the curmudgeon review JSON. For each entry, look up the iss_id in open-issues.json. If still open, the carry-over enforcement gate applies: you MUST take one of three actions in this run — patch, wontfix-with-rationale, or escalate to pending-human. Skipping a carry-over without recorded action is a self-test failure that should fail your run report.'

patches_and_selfapply_side.change: Add: 'Before declaring a self-applied-patch run complete: walk all unresolved_prior_cycle entries from any curmudgeon reviews you read this run. Verify each has a recorded terminal action (patch | wontfix-with-rationale | pending-human escalation). If any are missing, do NOT mark the run complete — return to triage.'

burndown_vs_bau_mode_design.summary: Two-mode toggle. BAU = steady-state with conservative caps. BURNDOWN = elevated caps + tighter safety controls (dry-run-by-default, ledger-required) to clear an existing accumulation safely fast.

primary.writers: Operator (direct edit via clone) OR decider's auto-revert logic
primary.readers: Decider (every run, in Step 0b setup) — sets local `K_cap`, `dryrun_default`, `ledger_required` accordingly
primary.rationale: Single source of truth. Schema-conformant. Survives FUSE staleness because decider clones fresh each run. Operator can flip with one edit; no HNOTE plumbing needed for the common case.
primary.ownership_classification: git-owned. Add to `build.js` OWNERSHIP and `workspace-sync.md` NEVER_PUSH.

engagement_mechanism_Q2.escape_hatch: HNOTE in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with action='set_decider_mode' for one-shot toggles when operator wants the decider to flip on its NEXT run without manually editing the flag file. Decider's Step E1 (mode-toggle reading) is extended to also handle set_decider_mode.

rejected_options.field_in_priority_queue.json: Conflates with curmudgeon's queue mode. Cleaner to separate.
rejected_options.tinker_DIRECTIVE_only: Too slow — operator needs same-run engagement for time-sensitive burn-down.
rejected_options.flag_in_status.json: status.json is workspace-owned; FUSE staleness risk.

auto_revert_logic_Q2.primary_condition: count(open issues with status='open') < 50 for 3 CONSECUTIVE decider runs → auto-revert burndown→bau. 3-run window prevents flapping at the threshold.
auto_revert_logic_Q2.secondary_condition: elapsed > 7 days in burndown → auto-revert. Hard time cap prevents indefinite burndown if operator forgets.
auto_revert_logic_Q2.tertiary_condition: 5 consecutive runs with zero closures (no more low-hanging fruit) → auto-revert. Recognizes saturation: 'whatever's left needs human attention.'
auto_revert_logic_Q2.manual_revert: Operator edits decider-mode.json directly OR HNOTE action='set_decider_mode' with mode='bau'.
auto_revert_logic_Q2.logging_on_revert: Decider writes to monitor/decisions/decider-mode-history.jsonl (append-only): { revert_at, from, to, trigger, open_count_at_revert, run_id }

control_a_closure_ledger.ownership_classification: git-owned (decider writes; never round-trip from FUSE). Add to build.js OWNERSHIP and workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH.

control_b_severity_tier_cap.rule: BURNDOWN auto-close is restricted to severity ∈ {minor, info}. Moderate gets pending-human routing (NOT auto-close). Major/critical untouched. Same matrix as M1's severity tier action matrix.
control_b_severity_tier_cap.rationale: Most of the backlog (160/231 = 69%) is minor severity. Even with a tight cap, 30/run × 6 runs/day × 60% close rate = ~108/day on minors alone. Don't risk wrong-closing a moderate to gain marginal speed.

control_c_recently_touched_guard.rule: Never auto-close an ISS modified in the last 48 hours. Read `last_modified` if present; else fall back to `found_at` plus most-recent-status-change timestamp.
control_c_recently_touched_guard.rationale: If an ISS was touched recently, someone (curmudgeon, decider, operator) is paying attention to it. Don't yank it out from under them.

control_d_dryrun_phase_1.rule: First 24 elapsed hours after burndown engages: M1 and M2 produce CANDIDATE-CLOSURE entries to closure-ledger.jsonl with `dryrun: true`. NO actual closures. Operator inspects ledger, approves batch via HNOTE action='approve_burndown_batch' OR rejects via 'cancel_burndown'. After approval, dryrun flag flips false on subsequent runs.
control_d_dryrun_phase_1.rationale: First-run paranoia. The blast radius of a misconfigured M2 is dozens of zombie-closes. Dryrun-then-approve is cheap insurance.

control_e_M3_unaffected_by_burndown_caps.rule: M3 (carry-over enforcement) runs in BOTH modes equally — it's gated by the curmudgeon writing `unresolved_prior_cycle`, not by per-run cap.
control_e_M3_unaffected_by_burndown_caps.rationale: Carry-overs are bounded by the curmudgeon's current run output, which is naturally small (~3-8 holes per review). No need to cap them; the bound is upstream.

BAU_caps_recommended.M2: unbounded — bound by EXP integration count per run, naturally ~1-3

estimated_effective_close_rate.M2_per_day: EXP integrations are typically 1-3/day in healthy state; each catches ~3-5 orphans at 60% guard-pass-rate; ~5-15/day
estimated_effective_close_rate.M3_per_day: Carry-overs in current corpus: ~10-15 detected; on a steady cadence ~1-3/day after backlog drains
estimated_effective_close_rate.days_to_clear_to_50: (231 − 50) / 125 = ~1.5 days at saturation; allow 3-5 days realistic accounting for context budget and run cadence

rate_estimate_Q4.is_this_safely_fast_question: Yes — the rate is bounded by guard-pass rate (60%), not by raw cap. Increasing caps further only shifts work earlier; doesn't change wrong-close rate. The bottleneck is review thoroughness, not throughput.

curmudgeon_interaction_during_burndown_Q.recommendation: Curmudgeon keeps running normally — DO NOT pause/throttle.
rationale: (a) The 'one-step-forward-one-step-back' worry assumes curmudgeon-created ISSs immediately age. With M3 (carry-over enforcement) in place, new curmudgeon ISSs that get re-flagged in a later cycle are forced through the M3 gate — they don't accumulate.
(b) Pausing the curmudgeon during burn-down creates a coverage hole: the bridge stops being painted. Bad for the review's primary mission.
(c) The cap on M1 is per-decider-run, not pipeline-global. Curmudgeon's output is bounded by its own scheduling, separately.
curmudgeon_interaction_during_burndown_Q.edge_case: If burn-down is engaged during a curmudgeon churn-and-burn period (curmudgeon at 30m cadence per CLAUDE.md), curmudgeon may temporarily out-create ISSs faster than M1 can close them. This is fine — burndown's auto-revert at count<50 simply takes longer. No special handling needed.

rollback_procedure_Q3_continued.principle: EVERY auto-closure is recorded in closure-ledger.jsonl with `can_revert: true`. The revert script reads ledger entries by run_id and re-opens the listed ISSs.
script_logic: 1. Open closure-ledger.jsonl, filter lines where closed_by_run === <run_id>.
2. For each: load closed-issues.json, find the matching iss_id in `closed`, remove it, append to open-issues.json with prior_status restored.
3. Append revert record to closure-ledger.jsonl (single line per revert: { reverted_at, reverted_by_run, original_close_run, iss_ids: [...] }) — preserves audit trail.
4. With --dry-run: print what WOULD be reverted, do not write.
rollback_test_cases: Single-run revert: revert all M1+M2 closures from run X.
Cross-run revert: revert all M2 closures across all runs in last 24h (filter by closed_at).
Selective revert: revert one specific ISS by id (script accepts --iss ISS-NNN).
Idempotency: running revert twice on same run_id is a no-op after first run.

Q1_mechanism_scope.rationale: Each addresses a different leak mode. M2 alone catches ~10-20 orphans (the 88-EXP-mention claim is misleading because of false positives). M3 alone catches ~10-15 carry-overs. M1 alone is the durable safety net but is conservative on auto-close. Combined, they cover the three structural leaks; without any one, that leak persists.
Q1_mechanism_scope.if_subset_required: If exactly one: M1 (durable, catches the long tail). If two: M1+M2 (M3 has the smallest backlog impact and can land later).

Q2_burndown_trigger_and_revert.engagement: Flag file monitor/decisions/decider-mode.json with mode='burndown'|'bau'. Operator HNOTE escape hatch.
Q2_burndown_trigger_and_revert.revert: Auto-revert when count(open) < 50 for 3 consecutive runs OR elapsed > 7 days OR 5 consecutive runs with zero closures, whichever fires first. Manual revert always available.

Q3_safety_controls.recommended_set: All five: closure-ledger (revertable), severity tier (auto-close minors only), recently-touched guard (no closures within 48h of last modification), dry-run for first 24h after burndown engages, M3 unaffected by caps.
Q3_safety_controls.any_additional: None required for first deployment. After 1-2 weeks of operation, may add: (a) closure-rate alarming (Tinker flags if M2 closure rate exceeds 30/run sustained — could indicate guard regression), (b) operator-approval gate for moderate severity (currently moderate gets pending-human routing, not auto-close — sufficient for now).

Q4_caps.justification: Context budget: each M1 candidate is ~50-200 lines of context (issue body + grep + decision); 30 candidates ≈ 3-6KB of context — fits in a typical decider run with headroom. 60% effective close rate keeps wrong-close rate <2% under the guards.

operator_questions_full_answers.Q5_curmudgeon_interaction: No pause/throttle. M3 prevents the one-step-forward-one-back churn structurally. See M5 detailed answer above.
operator_questions_full_answers.Q6_M2_false_positive_guard: Three-rule conjunctive gate: severity ∈ {minor,info}, no negative-signal substring, source not under monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/. Six worked examples (3 SAFE, 3 NOT) provided in M2 section. Yields ~10-20 clean closures from the current 90 EXP-mentioning open issues; the rest route through M1 or stay open for major-severity operator review.
operator_questions_full_answers.Q7_M3_schema: Add structured field `unresolved_prior_cycle: [{iss_id, prior_cycle, holes_index}]` on curmudgeon review JSON. Decider reads structured field first, falls back to free-text grep for legacy reviews. Migration: no backfill needed — legacy reviews continue working via fallback; new reviews use the structured field automatically.

Q8_implementation_order.recommendation: Order C (phased with burn-down first phase)
Q8_implementation_order.phase_1_24_to_72_hours: Land M2 + decider-mode.json + closure-ledger.jsonl + revert script + 24h dry-run-by-default. Operator inspects dry-run ledger, approves first batch.
Q8_implementation_order.phase_2_after_phase_1_clean: Land M1 + M3. Operator decides whether to re-engage burndown or let M1 BAU drain the residue (≈170 items left after Phase 1's M2 closes ~10-20 EXP-tied items at the front).
Q8_implementation_order.rationale: Phased risk. M2 is the highest-confidence mechanism (clearest signal: EXP integrated AND ISS passes guard); landing it first establishes operator trust in the closure-ledger / dry-run / revert workflow before M1's broader sweep engages. M3 is structurally minor (~15 items); pairs naturally with M1 in Phase 2.

Q9_24_stale_statused_entries.recommendation: One-shot archiver in Phase 1 deployment, scoped tightly.
Q9_24_stale_statused_entries.rule: status ∈ {closed, fixed, wontfix} → archive to closed-issues.json immediately (these statuses already say 'this is done'). Total: 2+9+3=14 entries.
Q9_24_stale_statused_entries.leave_in_place: status ∈ {monitoring, blocked-on-curmudgeon, assigned-analyst, partially-fixed-pending-verification, pending-human} → leave in place; these are truly active states, not 'done-but-not-archived'. Total: 2+4+3+1+2=12 entries.
Q9_24_stale_statused_entries.rationale: Archive only the obviously-terminal statuses. The 'active-but-non-open' statuses describe live waiting states (monitoring an external claim, blocked on curmudgeon's next cycle, etc.) — they belong in open-issues.json as a working set even if they aren't `status:'open'`.
Q9_24_stale_statused_entries.implementation: One-shot script `build-scripts/archive-terminal-status.js`, idempotent, safe to re-run. Operator triggers via direct invocation or HNOTE.

implementation_path_phase_1.duration: 2-3 hours of operator+decider coordination, primarily over a 24-72h dry-run window

files_to_modify.change: Add M2 EXP-tied auto-close addendum (false-positive guard + closure-ledger write)
Add: read decider-mode.json at run start, set local K_cap, dryrun_default, ledger_required
Extend to also handle action='set_decider_mode' (alongside set_curmudgeon_mode)
Add canonical schemas; update File Ownership table reference
Add decider-mode.json + closure-ledger.jsonl to git-owned table; update build.js OWNERSHIP and workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH per the duplicate-edit rule

files_to_create.initial_content: { "mode": "bau", "set_at": "<land-time ISO>", "set_by": "operator (PROP-026 Phase 1 deploy)", "reason": "initial state — burndown engaged manually after Phase 1 dry-run approval", "auto_revert_after": null, "auto_revert_when_open_below": 50 }
(empty file — appended to by decider runs)
Revert script per the rollback_procedure design

deployment_steps: 1. Author and review code changes in clean clone.
2. Run tests/run.sh (per CLAUDE.md commit gate). All must pass.
3. Operator commits Phase 1 changes (M2 + mode infrastructure + ledger + revert script + Q9 archiver).
4. Operator manually flips decider-mode.json to mode='burndown' with auto_revert_when_open_below=50.
5. Decider's next run reads burndown mode, runs M2 in DRYRUN — writes candidate-closure entries to ledger but does not actually close.
6. After 24h of dry-run accumulation (~5-10 ledger entries), operator inspects ledger.
7. Operator approves batch via HNOTE 'approve_burndown_batch' OR cancels via 'cancel_burndown'.
8. On approval, dryrun flag flips. Subsequent decider runs perform actual closures using the same guards.
implementation_path_phase_1.rollback_if_phase_1_misbehaves: node build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js --run <bad_run_id>. Manually flip decider-mode.json back to 'bau' (auto-revert also fires when count drops below 50; manual flip is faster).

files_to_modify.change: Add new Priority 5b — Stale-Issue Sweep (M1) section per the M1 plug-in skeleton

deployment_steps: 4. Operator decides: re-engage burndown (faster drain of residue) or stay BAU (slower, lower-risk drain).
5. Auto-revert at count<50 takes over once threshold met.

expected_outcome.day_zero_state: 231 open issues, 26 non-open-statused entries, 27 undated entries needing schema cleanup (separate PROP).
expected_outcome.day_three_phase_1_complete: Open count: ~210-220 (M2 closes ~10-20 EXP-tied orphans + Q9 archiver moves ~14 terminal-status entries to closed-issues.json). Net visible reduction in open-issues.json: ~25-30 entries. ISS-708 (the 30d-tomorrow item) routed through pending-human escalation if M1 isn't yet live.
expected_outcome.day_seven_phase_2_landed_burndown_engage: Open count: ~50-60. M1 has chewed through the ≤21d cohort + the older minors. M3 has cleared ~10-15 carry-overs. Auto-revert fires when count drops below 50.
expected_outcome.day_fourteen_steady_state: Open count: stable around 30-50 (BAU cap of 10/run for M1 + naturally-bounded M2/M3 maintains equilibrium with curmudgeon's incoming rate).
expected_outcome.obe_protection: ISS-708 will cross 30d on 2026-05-11 (tomorrow). If Phase 1 deploys today, M1 isn't live yet to catch it — operator-side action: route ISS-708 manually to pending-human before Phase 1 deploys, OR accept the 30d crossover as a one-time data point.

tests_required.test_name: M2 false-positive guard correctness on the 6 worked examples
tests_required.assertion: Given the 6 ISSs in worked_examples_from_current_open_issues, M2's classifier returns AUTO-CLOSE for ISS-1547 and ISS-1080, and DO_NOT_AUTO_CLOSE for the other 4.
Closing 5 ISSs, then reverting via revert script, restores exact prior state of open-issues.json + closed-issues.json.
decider-mode.json is read at Step 0b; HNOTE action='set_decider_mode' applied at Step E1 takes precedence and persists across runs.
If decider sees a curmudgeon review with unresolved_prior_cycle entries and finishes a run without taking terminal action on each, the run report self-test fails.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: M2 false-positive guard mis-classifies a borderline case (e.g., ISS-1080 stylistic disagreement)
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Closure-ledger + revert script. Operator can revert any individual closure. Dry-run for first 24h gives operator time to spot patterns before any actual closure happens.
risks_and_mitigations.residual: Low. The dry-run window is the load-bearing safeguard.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: M1's auto-wontfix on minor-severity stale issues hides a genuinely-still-real problem
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Severity tier matrix explicitly routes ambiguous cases to operator escalation, not auto-wontfix. The 'is this still real' signal triangulates re-grep + curmudgeon-recent-touch + EXP-status before any wontfix decision.
risks_and_mitigations.residual: Moderate. M1's effective close rate (60%) reflects this conservatism — only the high-confidence cases auto-close.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: M3 schema bump breaks legacy curmudgeon reviews that don't carry the unresolved_prior_cycle field
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Free-text grep fallback parses 'still open from c-N' patterns from holes_found descriptions. New reviews use structured field; old reviews continue working.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Burndown mode runs too long because curmudgeon keeps creating new ISSs faster than M1 can close
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Auto-revert triggers (count<50, elapsed>7d, 5-consecutive-zero-closure runs) all bound the burndown duration independently. The 7d hard cap is the ultimate safety.
Append-only by design; rotated annually by tinker into closure-ledger-archive-YYYY.jsonl. No mid-stream cleanup needed.
risks_and_mitigations.residual: Negligible — file grows ~1-2 lines/day after backlog drains.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Operator forgets to flip burndown→bau and elevated cap stays engaged in steady state, leading to over-aggressive closures
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Auto-revert at count<50 fires regardless. Manual operator flip is also one edit.

out_of_scope_deferred_to_separate_props: Schema cleanup of the 27 undated open issues (no found_at/found_date) — flagged in context_verification, deferred to PROP-NNN-schema-cleanup
Refactoring the curmudgeon's holes_found 'still open from c-N' free-text into structured form (preserving audit trail) — M3 fallback handles this; structural refactor is optional
Post-PROP-026 closure-rate alarming in tinker reports — would land 1-2 weeks after Phase 2 once steady-state rates are observable
verification_pattern: Tinker on next Mode 1 run checks: (a) decider-mode.json exists in monitor/decisions/, (b) closure-ledger.jsonl exists, (c) at least one M2 closure has been recorded if any EXP integrated since Phase 1 deployed, (d) open-issues count trajectory is monotonically downward in Phase 2, (e) revert script runs successfully on any single ledger entry without error.
follow_up_priors_set_by_this_proposal: Tinker should re-audit 14 days after Phase 2 deployment: is the steady-state open count stable around 30-50? Are auto-close rates within projected ranges?
Operator-facing dashboard hook (optional): a nightly script that emits open-issues count + age distribution to monitor/integrity/. Lets operator spot drift without reading raw JSON.
